Zh: “听你这么说，我很高兴。”警督已经习惯了你奇怪的咕哝声。他说这句话的时候，身上明显没有服用麻醉剂的迹象。这是好事。

En: That's wonderful to hear. The young woman takes a sip of coffee. Good un-narcotic coffee. Wholesome to look at.
Zh: “听你这么说，我很高兴。”年轻女子抿了一口咖啡。上好的、不含麻醉剂的咖啡。看起来非常健康。

En: That's an odd thing to say to yourself, officer. The young woman takes a sip of coffee. It does not seem to have narcotics in it.
Zh: “对自己说这种话挺奇怪的，警官。”年轻女子抿了一口咖啡。里面好像没放麻醉剂。

En: I seem to have a slight speed habit myself, though. Is this really a good idea?
Zh: 不过，我自己好像都有点嗑思必得的习惯。这真是个好主意吗？

En: And my proclivity for smoking doesn't hurt?
Zh: 那我抽烟的癖好没什么害处吧？

En: My drinking problem... isn't going to be a problem, is it?
Zh: 我的酗酒问题……应该不算什么问题，对吧？

En: Your words sound hollow and unconvincing. You're a weakling, a servant to the dark arts of narcomania.
Zh: 你的言语听起来空洞又牵强。你是一个弱者，是诚服于麻醉剂瘾那黑暗艺术的奴隶。

En: You say the words loudly to yourself. That makes them very convincing. The world applauds you -- you are finally on the holy warpath against narcomania!
Zh: 你对自己大声说到。这样听起来更有说服力一些。那些话语称赞着你——你总算踏上了对抗麻醉剂瘾的神圣之路！

En: Signage is put up and taken down, shop windows boarded up and reopened once more. What remains constant -- voyeurism, always unhelpful. And, at times, quite fully helpless.
Zh: 招牌竖起又被拆下，商店橱窗关闭又被重新打开。唯一永恒不变的——只有偷窥狂，从来都没什么用。而且有时候，是彻头彻尾的一无是处。

En: Is that why that hole in the wall is there? Voyeurism?
Zh: 所以这就是墙上有洞的原因？有偷窥狂？

En: It doesn't even matter why it was put there in the first place. That's something it could have been used for.
Zh: 其实它为什么一开始就在那里根本就不重要。也许之前就是那样用的吧。

En: [Finish thought.]
Zh: [完成思维。]

En: [Ignore thought.]
Zh: [忽略思维。]

En: It's not my fault this pinball operation failed.
Zh: 弹球操作失败不是我的错。

En: No, but how does it make you feel, to dig through its trash?
Zh: 不，但是在垃圾堆里乱翻的你有什么感受？

En: Like I should be grieving something, but I'm not sure what.
Zh: 感觉我应该为某种东西感到悲痛，但不确定是什么。

En: Like a good investigator.
Zh: 像一个优秀的调查员。

En: Like a survivor.
Zh: 像一个幸存者。

En: Like an intruder.
Zh: 像一个入侵者。

En: But why? None of this belongs to anyone anymore.
Zh: 但是为什么呢？这些东西已经不属于任何人了。

En: There are too many places to start, aren't there.
Zh: 有太多地方可以开始了，是吧。

En: Then do your job.
Zh: 那就赶紧做事吧。

En: But of what conflict or catastrophe?
Zh: 但是，到底是什么冲突，或者是大灾难呢？

En: You can almost *see* the shape of a man and a woman, writhing inside -- bathed in drug sweat and dirty linens. Bottles lie around everywhere...
Zh: 你几乎可以*看见*一个男人和一个女人的形状，在里面纠缠——沐浴在毒品，汗水和肮脏的床单之中。瓶子在地上散落的到处都是……

En: I think I can see into Klaasje's bedroom from here.
Zh: “我好像能从这里看到卡拉洁的卧室。”

En: What were those people doing -- in there?
Zh: 那些人在里面做什么？

En: [Finish thought.]
Zh: [完成思维。]

En: You lean closer to the peephole, instinctively. I bet they're doing something quite *unnatural* there. Sensationally unnatural.
Zh: 你本能地靠近了窥视孔。我肯定他们在做一些*不自然*的事，反常到骇人听闻。

En: If your mesolimbic reward pathway were to venture a guess: one of the bottles is probably being *put* somewhere it doesn't belong.
Zh: 如果你的中脑缘奖赏回路大胆猜测的话：其中一个瓶子可能被*放在*了不属于它的某个地方。

En: And were your glands to venture *another* guess -- they would add that *another* bottle is being put somewhere very close to that. The bottle is a bit smaller you see.
Zh: 然后你的腺体又大胆的做出*另一种*假设——他们还会加上*另一个*瓶子，就放在离之前那个不远的地方。这次看见的瓶子要稍微小一些。

En: Also! You can literally put speed up your asshole, did you know? Enters the bloodstream right through the mucus membrane! So yeah, that's definitely *also* taking place.
Zh: 还有！你可以把思必得塞进自己的直肠，我是说字面意义上的，懂吗？通过粘膜直接进入血液！没错，肯定*也*发生这种事了。

En: He leans closer to inspect the peephole. "You can barely see through. Better not to jump to *sensationalist* conclusions here."
Zh: 他靠近窥视孔。“几乎什么都看不见。最好不要仓促地*凭感觉*做出结论。”

En: The footprints on the floor, however... He points to the marks nearby, on the floor. "*Definitely* suspicious."
Zh: “不过，地板上的脚印……”他指着附近地板上的印记。“*确实*非常可疑。”

En: Boring footprints. I want to jump to sensationalist conclusions.
Zh: 无聊的脚印。我想直接凭感觉作出结论了。

En: Stupid superstition. But still -- it would be *interesting* to see what the cafeteria manager thinks of this...
Zh: 愚蠢的迷信。不过——看看餐厅经理对此有什么看法，应该*挺有意思*的……

En: It's not a ghost story. It's a curse -- and Garte ought be made knowledgeable so he can perform counter-spells.
Zh: 这不是什么鬼故事。这是个诅咒——而且加尔特应该知道这一点，这样他就能使用反制咒语了。

En: The lieutenant looks around the dusty, crowded room, inspecting the tools on the shelf.
Zh: 警督四下看了看这间灰尘扑扑又拥挤的小房间，检查着架子上的工具。

En: So this is where they brought faulty pinball machines to fix them up -- a long time ago. Everything is covered with dust now...
Zh: 看来他们就是把坏掉的弹球机带到这里来维修的——很久之前是。现在这里落了一层灰……

En: This seems to be some kind of workshop. For fixing up pinball machines -- a long time ago. Everything is covered with dust now...
Zh: 这里像是某种工坊。用来维修弹球机——很久之前是。现在这里落了一层灰……

En: Looks like they gave up on fixing the pinball machines at some point.
Zh: 看来他们在某个时候放弃修理弹球机了。

En: Looks like they gave up on fixing the pinball machines at some point.
Zh: “看来他们在某个时刻放弃维修弹球机了。”

En: At some point -- twenty years ago? Fifteen maybe. Before pinball went out of vogue.
Zh: 他表示赞同。“某个时刻——20年前吗？也许是15年前。在弹球变得不再流行之前。”

En: Doesn't look like that pinball machine will ever get fixed.
Zh: 看来那个弹球机应该是修不好了。

En: Doesn't look like that pinball machine will ever get fixed.
Zh: “看来那个弹球机应该是修不好了。”

En: This used to be a... pinball workshop.
Zh: 这里曾经是个……弹球工坊。

En: This used to be a... pinball workshop.
Zh: “这里曾经是个……弹球工坊。”

En: [Finish thought.]
Zh: [完成思维。]

En: Not interested. [Discard thought.]
Zh: 不感兴趣。[放弃思维。]

En: Looks like it. I'm guessing Martinaise North 22 used to be a pinball arcade before it became a hostel. There are machines left over... He taps his foot.
Zh: “看起来是。我猜马丁内斯北街22号在变成一间旅社之前，曾经是个弹球游戏室。还有些留下来的机器……”他抬了抬脚。

En: A creak, some dust falls off a shelf.
Zh: 一阵嘎吱声响过，架子上落下一些灰尘。

En: Downstairs in the hall -- next to the main door. One of them even works. I've seen one of the Hardies bang away at it.
Zh: “楼下大厅——就在正门旁边。有一台还能用呢。我见过一个哈迪兄弟的人咚咚咚地玩个不停。”

En: Remember the *Dicemaker*?! Then that means...
Zh: 还记得*骰子制造商*吗？！那也就是说……

En: Whirling-in-Rags was once the East Delta Pinball arcade. This is all left over from that.
Zh: “褴褛飞旋曾经是东德尔塔的弹球游戏室。这就的东西都是当时剩下的。”

En: Ah, yes -- as the novelty dicemaker said. He makes a note in his notebook. "This has absolutely nothing to do with the case, I'm sure. But I do like a nice little *connection*."
Zh: “啊，是的——就像新奇骰子匠人说的。”他往本子里记了几笔。“这绝对跟案子无关，我很肯定。不过我确实喜欢有那么一点点*关系*。”

En: Could this mean the Whirling-in-Rags really *is* part of the Doomed Commercial Area?
Zh: 这是不是意味着褴褛飞旋*真是*诅咒商业区的一部分？”

En: Could this mean the Whirling-in-Rags really *is* part of the Doomed Commercial Area?
Zh: “是不是说褴褛飞旋*真是*诅咒商业区的一部分？”

En: If that's true then our *cafeteria manager* is not going to like it. He looks around. "We should tell him what we found up here -- *omitting* that suspicion."
Zh: “如果是真的，那我们的*餐厅经理*肯定不会喜欢的。”他看了看周围。“我们应该把这里的发现告诉他——*省略*掉其中怀疑的部分。”

En: But then it went bankrupt... your skin crawls from making the connection!
Zh: 但是之后它破产了……你因为这种联想起了鸡皮疙瘩！

En: He does not appear to be the kind of man who likes his establishment to be part of a neighbourhood ghost story. About *bankruptcy*.
Zh: “自己的产业曾经是坊间鬼故事的一部分……他不像喜欢这种事的人。特别是*破产*。”

En: Yes. Pinball hasn't enjoyed a heyday for fifteen or twenty years.
Zh: 没错。弹球已经有15或者20年没有享受过全盛期了。

En: Yes. Martinaise North 22 used to be a pinball arcade.
Zh: 是的。马丁内斯北街22号曾经是个弹球游戏室。

En: And it has been forgotten. Now, there is only one working machine in the main hall -- the rest is lost to dust.
Zh: 而且它被遗忘了。现在大厅里只剩下一台可以运作的机器——其他的全都遗忘在灰尘之中。

En: As the Dicemaker said, yes; it all fits. Not that it has anything to do with the case, but it is a nice connection!
Zh: 跟骰子匠人说的一样，是的；一切都对上了。并不是说和案子有什么关系，但确实是个很好的关联！

En: Yes. It would mean that.
Zh: 是的，也许是这个意思。

En: And Garte would not like that. He should probably be made aware that his establishment is part of the neighbourhood ghost story.
Zh: 而且加尔特是不会喜欢的。也许应该告诉他，他的产业曾经是坊间鬼故事的一部分。
